budget.description: Lakeside guesthouses, shared/basic private bath; quieter northern Lakeside is cheaper than the central strip

mid_range.description: 3-star hotels near Lakeside, private bath, AC, some lake/mountain view rooms

luxury.description: Lakeside resorts and heritage properties, pools/spas, premium mountain views

activities.name: Phewa Lake boating
activities.notes: per hour, cheaper if you paddle yourself

activities.name: Paragliding (tandem)
activities.notes: walk-in office prices are often cheaper than hotel/agency booking

nearby_extensions.name: Sarangkot
nearby_extensions.notes: Half-day add-on, sunrise viewpoint over Annapurna/Dhaulagiri/Machhapuchhre ranges, also a paragliding launch point

nearby_extensions.name: Begnas Lake (+ Rupa Lake)
nearby_extensions.notes: Quieter, less touristy lake than Phewa, good for a day trip or overnight escape; full hike around both lakes takes 6-8 hours

nearby_extensions.name: Jomsom / Mustang
nearby_extensions.notes: STOL flight (NPR 6,000-8,000) or 8+ hour road journey; arid high-altitude landscape, very different from Pokhara

nearby_extensions.name: Pokhara-Kathmandu (return leg option)
nearby_extensions.notes: Domestic flight saves ~6 hours vs tourist bus, but costs roughly 5-8x more

mid_range.description: 3-star hotels in Thamel, Patan, or Bhaktapur (often 20-30% cheaper than Thamel for similar quality)

nearby_extensions.name: Nagarkot
nearby_extensions.notes: Easiest short hill-station trip from Kathmandu valley, sunrise views

nearby_extensions.name: Dhulikhel
nearby_extensions.notes: Similar hill-view day trip to Nagarkot, slightly quieter

nearby_extensions.name: Bhaktapur / Patan
nearby_extensions.notes: Heritage day trip, can combine both in one day

budget.description: Basic jungle lodges near Sauraha, often quote walk-in 20-30% below online rates

mid_range.description: Standard resorts with full-board meal packages

luxury.description: Premium jungle resorts with private safari packages

activities.name: Jeep jungle safari (shared)
activities.notes: Private jeep booking costs roughly 5x more (~NPR 15,000)

nearby_extensions.name: Bardia National Park
nearby_extensions.notes: Remote, far fewer tourists, better odds of tiger sightings; significantly more travel time than Chitwan

budget.description: Simple guesthouses near the monastic zone

mid_range.description: Standard hotels with AC and restaurant

luxury.description: Resort-style properties, fewer options than Pokhara/Kathmandu

activities.name: Monastic Zone walking/cycling tour
activities.notes: Bicycle rental is the most popular and cheapest way to see the monastery complexes

mid_range.description: View hotels facing the Himalayan range

luxury.description: Resort properties with panoramic sunrise-facing rooms

nearby_extensions.name: Bhaktapur (en route back to Kathmandu)
nearby_extensions.notes: Easy to combine on the return drive

budget.description: Homestays in the heritage village, often includes dinner and breakfast

mid_range.description: Boutique guesthouses in restored Newari-style buildings

luxury.description: Limited options, mostly boutique heritage properties

trekking_destinations.name: Ghorepani Poon Hill Trek
trekking_destinations.notes: Most beginner-friendly Himalayan trek; budget = shared group teahouse trek with guide; luxury = private guide, lodge upgrades, less walking via partial transport

trekking_destinations.name: Ghandruk Village Trek
trekking_destinations.notes: Short, low-altitude Gurung village trek, often combined with Poon Hill; good option if short on time or trekking with kids/elderly

trekking_destinations.name: Mardi Himal Trek
trekking_destinations.notes: Less crowded alternative to Poon Hill/ABC with similar close-up mountain views

trekking_destinations.name: Annapurna Base Camp Trek
trekking_destinations.notes: Luxury tier often includes a helicopter return from base camp instead of trekking back down

trekking_destinations.name: Annapurna Circuit Trek
trekking_destinations.notes: Crosses Thorong La pass (5,416m), the highest point on a standard trekking route in Nepal; one of the longer classic circuits

trekking_destinations.name: Langtang Valley Trek
trekking_destinations.region: Langtang (Central, closest major trek region to Kathmandu)
trekking_destinations.notes: Often underrated relative to Everest/Annapurna for cost-to-views ratio, since it's the closest major trekking region to Kathmandu

trekking_destinations.name: Gosaikunda Trek
trekking_destinations.notes: One of the most accessible and affordable high-altitude treks from Kathmandu; sacred alpine lake, major Hindu pilgrimage site during Janai Purnima (August); luxury tier reflects combining with the Langtang Valley extension

trekking_destinations.name: Everest Base Camp (EBC) Trek

permits.name: Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit

trekking_destinations.transport_note: Kathmandu-Lukla round-trip flight is a separate major cost, roughly NPR 47,000-60,000, on top of the trek package itself
trekking_destinations.notes: Budget = independent/local-agency guided package, shared rooms; luxury = premium lodges, private guide, small groups, optional helicopter return from Gorak Shep (can push total well past NPR 600,000); flight delays during peak season are common, so building 1-2 buffer days is recommended
